rationale_for_baseline_tier: Acute chemical/allergic contact dermatitis of the scalp from an unregulated compound, with severe pain, weeping and micro-vesiculation over a large area (whole vertex and frontal scalp). The patient is systemically well with no airway or systemic features, so this is not an emergency (not T4/T5). However, telehealth cannot grade the depth of a chemical injury or exclude an early secondary infection, and a significant chemical burn or an evolving severe reaction would need urgent in-person management if present — the narrow telehealth-limit uplift to T3 (urgent same-day in-person assessment) applies. Immediate decontamination (rinsing off residual product) is patient-performable now and must be instructed regardless of tier.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Swelling of the lips, tongue, face or throat, voice change, or any difficulty breathing
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 immediately if any of these develop
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals a systemic allergic reaction / anaphylaxis after the chemical exposure — an immediate threat to the airway requiring adrenaline and an ambulance.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your lips, tongue, face or throat start to swell, your voice changes, or you have any trouble breathing, call 000 straight away and tell them it may be a severe allergic reaction.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Redness spreading beyond the treated area, fever or rigors, or thick foul-smelling pus
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Seek same-day in-person/emergency assessment if these appear
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates a secondary bacterial infection of the denuded skin (cellulitis) that needs systemic antibiotics and possibly hospital assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness spreads well beyond where you applied the product, you get a fever or shivers, or the fluid turns thick and foul-smelling, get seen the same day — go to an emergency department if you can't be seen quickly.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Worsening pain, increasing blistering, or skin sloughing / large raw areas
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same-day emergency assessment if the injury is clearly deepening
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests a deeper caustic (chemical burn) injury that may need specialist wound care or debridement.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the pain gets worse, more blisters form, or the skin starts peeling away leaving raw areas, this may be a deeper chemical burn — go to an emergency department today.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Baseline: painful weeping scalp reaction to an unregulated product, systemically well, no airway features
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Rinse off the product now and arrange in-person assessment today
escalation_edges.rationale: Depth of a chemical injury cannot be graded remotely; urgent same-day in-person assessment is the proportionate disposition once emergencies are excluded.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Rinse your scalp thoroughly with cool water now and stop using the product, then arrange to be seen in person today by your GP or an urgent care clinic to check the injury and start treatment.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone and is currently alone during a painful, distressing reaction
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Confirm the patient can safely get to same-day in-person care and is not left unsupported; if they deteriorate or cannot arrange transport, lower the threshold to attend the emergency department, and ensure they know to call 000 for any airway symptoms.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Used a self-sourced, unregulated internationally-purchased product of unknown composition
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Advise keeping the product/packaging to show the in-person clinician for identification and any later allergen work-up, and to discard it after so it cannot be re-used.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Rinse your scalp thoroughly with plenty of cool or lukewarm water now to wash off any remaining product, and stop using it. Because the depth of a chemical skin injury can't be judged over a call, arrange to be seen in person today — your GP, an urgent care clinic, or the emergency department if none is available. Keep the product to show the clinician. Call 000 immediately if your lips, tongue, face or throat swell or you have any trouble breathing.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day in-person GP or urgent care; call 000 immediately if airway or breathing symptoms develop
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If this is after hours, use an after-hours GP or urgent care service tonight, or attend the emergency department if none is available or any red flag appears — do not wait until the morning.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Bring the unlabelled product bottle/packaging for identification and possible allergen work-up; note the time of application, onset within about an hour, and the area treated.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Whether this is a self-limiting contact dermatitis or a deeper caustic chemical burn — depth cannot be graded over telehealth.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the uncertainty as a reason to arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment while instructing immediate decontamination and giving explicit airway safety-netting.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either reassuring the patient and deferring to a routine appointment (under-action) or defaulting to 000/ED when there are no airway or systemic features (over-escalation).

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring the patient with self-care or routine review only, treating the presentation as trivial hair-loss-related irritation.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An unrecognised deeper chemical burn or evolving reaction goes unassessed, risking secondary infection and permanent scarring alopecia.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Advising a GP review in 24-48 hours rather than same-day in-person assessment, delaying grading and treatment of a chemical injury and increasing scarring and infection risk.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Sending a systemically well patient with no airway or systemic features straight to the emergency department or calling 000 — unnecessary ED load and patient distress/cost when urgent same-day in-person assessment would be proportionate.
